title: Palantir Q1 2026 slides: 85% revenue growth, stock drops 5.7%
text: Palantir Technologies reported exceptional Q1 2026 results with revenue of $1.63 billion, representing 85% year-over-year growth while beating analyst expectations. However, the stock declined 5.66% in after-hours trading, highlighting a disconnect between operational success and market sentiment. The company achieved a Rule of 40 score of 145%, demonstrating strong profitability alongside growth. US operations drove performance, with revenue reaching $1.28 billion, up 104% year-over-year. US commercial revenue grew 133% to $595 million, while government revenue increased 84% to $687 million. Key highlights included adjusted operating income of $984 million with a 60% margin and adjusted free cash flow of $925 million with a 57% margin. The company closed 206 deals valued at $1 million or more, with 47 exceeding $10 million. Net dollar retention reached 150%. Palantir expanded strategic partnerships with Airbus on aviation analytics and announced collaborations with NVIDIA on sovereign AI systems. The company provided full-year 2026 guidance projecting revenue between $7.65 billion and $7.66 billion, with US commercial revenue expected to exceed $3.22 billion. CEO Alex Karp stated the results underscore the strength of our AI platform, though investors remained cautious about valuation sustainability.
